title: (No AI) Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
author: Andrew Liszewski
content:
![Solos’ AirGo A6 smart glasses in a clear shiny honey design.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/solos1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The AirGo A6 are available in multiple designs including several transparent color options. | Image: Solos

Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40 grams depending on the frame style, but the new AirGo A6 weigh around 19 grams. Part of the weight savings comes from thinner temple arms housing speakers, batteries, and other electronics. For comparison, the [new Meta Glasses announced last month](https://www.theverge.com/tech/954052/meta-glasses-hands-on-kylie-jenner-smart-glasses-price-battery-privacy) weigh around 54 to nearly 60 grams depending on the style.

Pricing and availability for the new AirGo A6 hasn't been finalized yet, but the smart glasses will support "full prescription lens compatibilit …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/961711/solos-airgo-a6-smart-glasses-ai-assistant-privacy)

The AirGo A6 are available in multiple designs including several transparent color options. | Image: Solos	

Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40 grams depending on the frame style, but the new AirGo A6 weigh around 19 grams. Part of the weight savings comes from thinner temple arms housing speakers, batteries, and other electronics. For comparison, the new Meta Glasses announced last month weigh around 54 to nearly 60 grams depending on the style.
Pricing and availability for the new AirGo A6 hasn't been finalized yet, but the smart glasses will support "full prescription lens compatibilit …

title: (No AI) I spy
author: Victoria Song
content:
![Senior reviewer Victoria Song wearing Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses while looking over her shoulder. The photo is lit so there are dramatic shadows across her face.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/10/257980_Meta_Ray-Ban_Display_AKrales_0111.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

I’m just doing my job, but turns out, it feels lousy holding other people’s privacy in your hands.

I've long argued that Hollywood has [simultaneously set](https://www.theverge.com/23678696/smart-glasses-spy-films-gadgets-privacy) and [ruined our expectations for smart glasses](https://gizmodo.com/video/how-science-fiction-ruined-smart-glasses). But after binge-watching two seasons of Netflix's _A Man on the Inside_, this is perhaps the first time I've seen Hollywood, perhaps inadvertently, illustrate the biggest cultural problem with smart glasses as they stand today.

In a nutshell, Ted Danson plays Charles Nieuwendyk, an elderly widower who finds a new purpose working for a private investigator. Armed with a pair of Ray-Ban Meta-like glasses, a voice recorder, and a smartphone, Nieuwendyk infiltrates a retirement home, and several privacy-infringing hijinks ensue as he hunts for t …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/column/961707/smart-glasses-ai-wearables-meta-surveillance-privacy)

title: (No AI) How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
content: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

title: (No AI) Core dump epidemiology: fixing an 18-year-old bug
content: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.
